title: Agalma octophyllum
taxonomy: Agalma octophyllum is a species of siphonophore, a type of colonial, carnivorous marine animal. It belongs to the phylum Cnidaria, which also includes jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones. Within the phylum Cnidaria, Agalma octophyllum is classified as a member of the class Hydrozoa. The class Hydrozoa is further divided into several orders, and Agalma octophyllum is specifically classified as a member of the order Siphonophorae. This classification reflects the animal's unique body structure, which consists of a network of specialized individuals working together to form a single organism.
